title: Agricultural Inspectors
description: Inspect agricultural commodities, processing equipment, and facilities, and fish and logging operations, to ensure compliance with regulations and laws governing health, quality, and safety.

tasks.text: Inspect food products and processing procedures to determine whether products are safe to eat.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Interpret and enforce government acts and regulations and explain required standards to agricultural workers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect agricultural commodities or related operations, as well as fish or logging operations, for compliance with laws and regulations governing health, quality, and safety.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Label and seal graded products and issue official grading certificates.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor the operations and sanitary conditions of slaughtering or meat processing plants.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Take emergency actions, such as closing production facilities, if product safety is compromised.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Verify that transportation and handling procedures meet regulatory requirements.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect the cleanliness and practices of establishment employees.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Examine, weigh, and measure commodities, such as poultry, eggs, meat, or seafood to certify qualities, grades, and weights.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect or test horticultural products or livestock to detect harmful diseases, chemical residues, or infestations and to determine the quality of products or animals.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Monitor the grading performed by company employees to verify conformance to standards.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Write reports of findings and recommendations and advise farmers, growers, or processors of corrective action to be taken.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Collect samples from animals, plants, or products and route them to laboratories for microbiological assessment, ingredient verification, or other testing.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide consultative services in areas such as equipment or product evaluation, plant construction or layout, or food safety systems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Compare product recipes with government-approved formulas or recipes to determine acceptability.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
